blocks:
Ayahs

هَلْ أَتَىٰ عَلَى ٱلْإِنسَـٰنِ حِينٌ مِّنَ ٱلدَّهْرِ لَمْ يَكُن شَيْـًٔا مَّذْكُورًا (۱)
Has there been a period of time for man when he was not anything worthy of mention? (1)
إِنَّا خَلَقْنَا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ مِن نُّطْفَةٍ أَمْشَاجٍ نَّبْتَلِيهِ فَجَعَلْنَـٰهُ سَمِيعًۢا بَصِيرًا (۲)
Indeed We created man from the drop of a mixed fluid so that We may put him to test, so We endowed with hearing and sight. (2)

Key Points

Dahr means time and era, and Amshāj is the plural of Mashīj, meaning mixed or mingled. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said in a narration: "كان شیئاً لم یكن مذكوراً" (191). Man was a thing, but He was not worthy of mention, and later He acquired worthiness. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام says: Man was in the Divine decree, but He was not an existing thing. "لم یكن شیئاً مذكوراً" (192). Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The meaning of "نطفة امشاج" (mingled sperm/drop) is the mixing of the sperm of the man with that of the woman (193).

To awaken a sleeping conscience, sometimes certain matters are presented in the form of a question. Just as a mother asks her child: Did I not breastfeed you? In this Ayah, too, the word Hal (هل) means Qad (قد). "هل اتى على الإنسان..." means: Verily, a period has passed over every human being during which He was not a thing worthy of mention.

Man's insignificance is sometimes natural, "لم یكن شیئاً مذكوراً," but sometimes, due to inappropriate performance, man reduces himself to the point of insignificance. As the Quran says: "يا أهل الكتاب لستم على شى ءٍ حتّى تقيموا التوراة والإنجيل و ..." O People of the Scripture! If you do not uphold the Torah and the Gospel and..., you are nothing (194).

The Quran mentions the creation of man from a drop of sperm 12 times, both to demonstrate the power of Allah, Who creates man from a drop of sperm, and so that man does not fall into arrogance and pride.

191) Tafsir Burhan. 192) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq. 193) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 194) Al-Ma'idah: 68.

Messages

  1. Man must reflect upon the origin of his existence. "لم یكن شیئاً مذكوراً"
  2. Man is a newly created and contingent being. "لم یكن شیئاً مذكورا"
  3. Man has followed an evolutionary process in his existential path and has passed through stages: initially, He did not exist, "لم یك شیئاً" (195); even after coming into existence, He was not a thing worthy of mention, "لم یكن شیئاً مذكوراً."
  4. Remembering weaknesses removes man's pride and makes him submit to Allah. "لم یكن شیئاً مذكورا"
  5. The creation of man from an insignificant drop of sperm is a manifestation of Divine greatness. "إنّا خلقنا الإنسان من نطفة..."
  6. Allah demonstrates His power by forming an image upon water. "من نطفة امشاج"
  7. The creation of man is wise and purposeful. "خلقنا الانسان ... نبتلیه"
  8. Man is a responsible and obligated being. (Testing is a sign of obligation/duty.) "نبتلیه"
  9. All human beings throughout history are subjected to Divine testing. "نبتلیه"
  10. Mentioning the two characteristics of hearing and sight is for example, otherwise all bodily organs are subjected to examination/testing. "سميعاً بصيراً"

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Insan

It is narrated regarding the Portrait of Surah Al-Insan: Imam Hassan and Imam Hussain علیهما السلام fell ill. The Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله came with the people to visit them and suggested to Hadrat Ali عليه السلام that He should vow to fast for the recovery of His two dear children. After the two Hassans recovered, Hadrat Ali and Hadrat Fatimah علیهما السلام fasted for three days, but they had no food in the house for Iftar (breaking the fast). Hadrat Ali عليه السلام borrowed some barley from Sham'un the Jew, and bread was prepared. At sunset, they first performed the Maghrib prayer and intended to break their fast, when on the first night, a needy person (miskin), on the second night, an orphan, and on the third night, a captive beggar knocked on the door of Ali's house عليه السلام and requested food. The Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام gave their food to them for the pleasure of Allah, and they themselves broke their fast with water. Almighty Allah revealed this Surah in description of their sacrifice. It is important that the Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله was not involved or a partner in this sacred act, so that it would be clear that this Surah was revealed only concerning the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام (190).

190) Al-Ghadir, Vol. 3, p. 107.

Ayahs

إِنَّا هَدَيْنَـٰهُ ٱلسَّبِيلَ إِمَّا شَاكِرًا وَإِمَّا كَفُورًا (۳)
Indeed We have guided him to the way, be he grateful or ungrateful. (3)
إِنَّآ أَعْتَدْنَا لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ سَلَـٰسِلَا۟ وَأَغْلَـٰلًا وَسَعِيرًا (٤)
Indeed for the faithless We have prepared chains, iron collars, and a blaze. (4)

Key Points

Guidance is of several types: Sometimes guidance is innate (fitrī). As the Quran states: «فالهمها فجورها و تقواها»(196) Allah inspired the human soul with its wickedness and its righteousness. Another type is guidance achieved through learning, intellect, consultation, experience, and study. And the third type is guidance that occurs through the propagation and instruction of the prophets and the vicegerents (awliyā). Gratitude (shukr) is sometimes heartfelt, meaning we acknowledge all blessings are from Allah; sometimes it is practical, meaning that a person uses Allah's blessings only in the way of truth (Haqq); and sometimes it is verbal, meaning a person utters "Al-Hamdu Lillahi Rabbil-'Alamin" (Praise be to Allah, Lord of the Worlds). Salāsil (سَلاسل) is the plural of silsilah (سِلسلة), meaning chain, and Aghlāl (اَغلال) is the plural of ghull (غُلّ), meaning a collar placed around the neck, and Sa'īr (سَعیر) means blazing fire. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding the verse «امّا شاكراً و امّا كفوراً»: «عرفناه اِمّا آخذ و اِمّا تارك»(197) We introduced the path to him, either he takes it or he leaves it. Salāsilan wa Aghlālan means chains on the feet and collars on the neck. In the path of servitude and gratitude toward Allah, man traverses a weak or moderate stage, but in the path of rebellion and disobedience, he traverses a high stage. In gratitude, he is shākir (grateful, not shakūr - extremely grateful), but in disbelief (kufr), he is kafūr (extremely ungrateful/disbelieving). «امّا شاكراً و امّا كفوراً» means his gratitude is ordinary, but his disbelief is strong. Pay attention to a few other examples. The Quran states: «انّه لیؤوس»(198) Man is certainly despairing. «انّ الانسان لظلوم»(199) Indeed, man is extremely unjust. «كان الانسان عجولاً»(200) Man is a hasty creature. «كان الانسان قتوراً»(201) Man is narrow-minded (or stingy). «انّ الانسان خلق هلوعاً»(202) Indeed, man was created anxious and impatient. «اذا مسّه الشر جزوعاً»(203) When evil touches him, he is extremely distressed and panicky. «انّ الانسان لربّه لكنود»(204) Indeed, man is extremely ungrateful and miserly toward his Lord.

195) Maryam, 67. 196) Ash-Shams, 8. 197) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq. 198) Hud, 9. 199) Ibrahim, 34. 200) Al-Isra, 11. 201) Al-Isra, 100. 202) Al-Ma'arij, 19. 203) Al-Ma'arij, 20. 204) Al-'Adiyat, 6.

Messages

1- The trial of man is based on awareness and freedom: Awareness through the eye, ear, intellect, inner nature (fitrah), and external guidance; and freedom based on the choice that has been given to all humans: «نبتلیه... سمیعاً بصیراً هدیناه... امّا شاكراً و امّا كفوراً»

2- Allah has completed the proof (Hujjah) upon man. «انّا هدیناه السبیل»

3- There is only one correct path. «انّا هدیناه السبیل» (The word sabīl is singular.)

4- Guidance is a blessing worthy of gratitude. «انّا هدیناه السبیل امّا شاكراً» (Treading the path of religion is divine gratitude, and deviation from it is ingratitude and thanklessness.)

5- Those who do not accept the guidance of the prophets have committed the highest stage of disbelief (kufr). (Instead of saying immā kāfiran, He said: «امّا شاكراً و امّا كفوراً». Because kafūr is the peak of disbelief.)

6- Whoever is not grateful (shākir) is extremely ungrateful (kafūr); there is no third way. «امّا شاكراً و امّا كفوراً»

7- In the education of man, the role of warning is greater than that of glad tidings. (Therefore, the Quran first warns and then gives glad tidings.) «انّا اعتدنا للكافرین...»

8- Divine punishments have a basis and standard. «اعتدنا للكافرین سلاسل و اغلالاً و سعیراً»

9- Every choice entails its own consequence. Disbelief leads to Hell, and gratitude leads to Paradise. «اِمّا شاكراً و اِمّا كفوراً... انّا اعتدنا للكافرین... انّ الأبرار...»

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلْأَبْرَارَ يَشْرَبُونَ مِن كَأْسٍ كَانَ مِزَاجُهَا كَافُورًا (٥)
Indeed the pious will drink from a cup seasoned with Kafur, (5)
عَيْنًا يَشْرَبُ بِهَا عِبَادُ ٱللَّهِ يُفَجِّرُونَهَا تَفْجِيرًا (٦)
a spring where Allah’s servants will drink, making it gush forth as they please. (6)

Key Points

Abrār (الأبرار) is either the plural of Barr (البَرّ) (like Arbāb (الأرباب) is the plural of Rabb (الرّبّ)) or the plural of Bārr (البارّ) (like Ashhād (الأشهاد) is the plural of Shāhid (الشاهد)). In any case, Barr (البَرّ) means vastness or expansiveness. The desert is called Barr because of its vastness. The righteous person possesses an open and expansive soul. His goal in performing good deeds is not to attain wealth or status, but rather He has a thought that transcends wealth and status. The hands of the righteous are open to help others, and their reward (ajr) is also vast. (205)

Kāfūr (كافور) means a fragrant plant that is proverbial for its coolness and whiteness. Tafjīr (تفجیر) means splitting or bursting forth. A person who tears the veil of modesty through sin is called a Fājir (فاجر), and Fajr (فجر) is the light that splits the darkness of the night.

Based on narrations, the intent of Abrār (الأبرار) in these verses refers to the Ahl al-Bayt (the Household of the Prophet) علیهم السلام, who fasted based on a vow and gave their iftār (meal for breaking the fast) to the orphan, the needy, and the captive, as mentioned in the subsequent verses.

Although some consider this Surah to be Medinan, some biased opponents say: Surah Al-Insan is Meccan, and the two Hasans (al-Hasan and al-Husayn) علیهما السلام were born in Medina. Therefore, these verses were not revealed concerning the Ahl al-Bayt, because their illness, the Prophet's visit, and the fasting of their parents took place in Medina!

In response, we say: In many Meccan Surahs, there are Medinan verses. The best evidence for this point is that the word asīr (أسیر - captive) appears in these verses, even though there were no captives in Mecca, and taking captives relates to the battles that occurred after the migration to Medina.

(205) Mufradāt al-Rāghib.

Messages

1- Benevolence and righteousness are manifestations of gratitude for Divine guidance. «امّا شاكراً... انّ الاَبرار...»

2- The drinks of Paradise are mixed with fragrant and perfumed substances. «كان مزاجها كافوراً»

3- The enjoyments of Paradise belong to those who have elevated themselves to the stage of servitude (‘ubūdiyyah). «یشرب بها عباد اللّه»

4- The temporary deprivations of Allah’s servants in this world will be compensated for on the Day of Judgment. «یشرب بها عباد اللّه»

5- The Ahl al-Bayt were titled Abrār (the Righteous) due to their care for the deprived, and they were titled ‘Ibād Allāh (Servants of Allah) due to their obedience to Allah. «انّ الابرار... عباد اللّه»

6- The servants of Allah are people who do good to others, and the righteous are servants of Allah. «الابرار یشربون... یشرب بها عباداللّه»

7- The will of the people of Paradise is effective; they attain whatever they desire, and whatever they will is brought to pass. «یفجرونها تفجیراً»

Ayahs

يُوفُونَ بِٱلنَّذْرِ وَيَخَافُونَ يَوْمًا كَانَ شَرُّهُۥ مُسْتَطِيرًا (۷)
They fulfill their vows and fear a day whose ill will be widespread. (7)
وَيُطْعِمُونَ ٱلطَّعَامَ عَلَىٰ حُبِّهِۦ مِسْكِينًا وَيَتِيمًا وَأَسِيرًا (۸)
For the love of Him, they feed the needy, the orphan and the prisoner, (8)
إِنَّمَا نُطْعِمُكُمْ لِوَجْهِ ٱللَّهِ لَا نُرِيدُ مِنكُمْ جَزَآءً وَلَا شُكُورًا (۹)
[saying,] ‘We feed you only for the sake of Allah. We desire no reward from you, nor thanks. (9)
إِنَّا نَخَافُ مِن رَّبِّنَا يَوْمًا عَبُوسًا قَمْطَرِيرًا (۱٠)
Indeed we fear a frowning and fateful day from our Lord.’ (10)

Key Points

"Mustaṭīr" (مُستطیر) is derived from "sayṭarah" (سَیطره), meaning dominance, and its root "ṭayr" (طَیر) means vastness or widespread.

"Qamṭarīr" (قَمطریر) means evil that is severe.

Based on some narrations, feeding the needy (miskīn), the orphan (yatīm), and the captive (asīr) occurred on a single night, while according to others, it happened over three consecutive nights, with one of them approaching each night.

The intent of "يطعمون الطعام على حبّه" (they feed food, despite their love for it) is that the Ahl al-Bayt (the Household of the Prophet) gave the food to others even though they themselves loved that food. Just as the Quran states elsewhere: "لن تنالوا البرّ حتّى تنفقوا ممّا تحبّون" (You will never attain piety until you spend from that which you love) (206).

The author of Tafsir al-Mizan states: The love of the Ahl al-Bayt for Allah is mentioned in the next verse where they say: "انّما نطعمكم لوجه اللّه" (We feed you only for the sake of Allah's Face), and the meaning of "على حبّه" (despite their love for it) refers to their attachment to the food. In this case, besides their sincerity (ikhlas), their self-sacrifice (īthār) is also proven.

If you do not seek fame and reputation, Allah will immortalize your name. The act performed by the Ahl al-Bayt did not have great material value, but because it was sincere, Allah made it everlasting.

In this verse, the words miskīn (needy), yatīm (orphan), and asīr (captive) are presented in the indefinite form (nakirah) to indicate that in feeding, one should not be selective, but rather give charity to whoever they may be.

Parvin E'tesami says regarding sincerity in charity:

A great man gave a beggar a single dirham, [Saying] "Remember us when you pray." Someone laughed and said, "This small dirham Was not worth this transaction and trade." Never show off your obedience, Do not make hypocrisy and pretense a veil for the heart. You do good to the needy and the poor, For goodness itself becomes the cause of prayer. At the time of giving and charity, Parvin, One should have nothing in the heart but Allah.

Fear of Allah means fear of His station (or presence). Just as He says elsewhere: "و لمن خاف مقام ربّه" (But for him who fears the standing before his Lord) (207).

(206) Tafsir Burhan; Mahasin Barqi, Vol. 2, p. 397. (207) Ar-Rahman, 46.

Messages

1- True perfection is achieved when goodness becomes institutionalized within a person. (All perfections are mentioned using the present tense verb, which indicates the continuity of the action.) "يوفون" (they fulfill), "يخافون" (they fear), "يطعمون" (they feed), etc.

2- Feeding and charity have greater value when the item given in charity is needed and desired by the person. "يطعمون الطعام على حبّه"

3- Islam is the protector of the poor, the orphans, and the captives. "و يطعمون الطعام... مسكينا و يتيما و أسيراً"

4- Some people love food, and some love feeding. "يطعمون الطعام على حبّه"

5- Feeding has greater value when it comes from oneself and is done by one's own hand. "نطعمكم" (We feed you)

6- Aid is valuable when it is sincere and free from any obligation or expectation. "نطعمكم لوجه اللّه"

7- What gives value to an action is the sincere motive and devotion (ikhlas) in the deed. "نطعمكم لوجه اللّه لا نرید منكم جزاء و لا شكورا"

8- The righteous not only do not ask for thanks verbally, but they do not even think about praise or thanks in their hearts. "لا نرید... شكوراً"

9- Although the righteous (abrār) do not expect reward, people should not be indifferent. In Ayah 25 of Surah Al-Qasas, we read that Prophet Shu'ayb sent his daughter to pay the reward for Prophet Moses' act of watering the flocks. "انّ ابى یدعوك لیجزیك اجر ما سقیت"

10- Waiving the reward alone is not a sign of sincerity; waiving praise and thanks is also necessary. "لا نرید منكم جزاءً و لا شكورا"

11- Fulfilling a vow (nadhir) is obligatory, and whoever does not fulfill his vow should fear Divine wrath. "يوفون بالنذر و يخافون يوماً"

12- If an action is for Allah, we fear only His wrath, not people. "نخاف من ربّنا"

13- Having a Divine motive does not contradict the desire for reward (thawāb) or the fear of punishment ('iqāb). This is because reward and punishment also come from Allah. "لوجه اللّه... انّا نخاف من ربّنا یوماً عبوساً"

14- On the Day of Judgment, not only will people be grim, but the Day itself will be grim. "يوماً عبوساً"

15- To escape that grim Day, do not be grim today toward the needy, the orphan, and the captive. "نخاف من ربّنا یوماً عبوساً"

Ayahs

فَوَقَىٰهُمُ ٱللَّهُ شَرَّ ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمِ وَلَقَّىٰهُمْ نَضْرَةً وَسُرُورًا (۱۱)
So Allah saved them from that day’s ills and graced them with freshness [on this faces] and joy [in their hearts]. (11)
وَجَزَىٰهُم بِمَا صَبَرُوا۟ جَنَّةً وَحَرِيرًا (۱۲)
He rewarded them for their patience with a garden and [garments of] silk, (12)
مُّتَّكِـِٔينَ فِيهَا عَلَى ٱلْأَرَآئِكِ ۖ لَا يَرَوْنَ فِيهَا شَمْسًا وَلَا زَمْهَرِيرًا (۱۳)
reclining therein on couches, without facing any [scorching] sun, or [biting] cold. (13)
وَدَانِيَةً عَلَيْهِمْ ظِلَـٰلُهَا وَذُلِّلَتْ قُطُوفُهَا تَذْلِيلًا (۱٤)
Its shades will be close over them and its clusters [of fruits] will be hanging low. (14)
وَيُطَافُ عَلَيْهِم بِـَٔانِيَةٍ مِّن فِضَّةٍ وَأَكْوَابٍ كَانَتْ قَوَارِيرَا۠ (۱٥)
They will be served around with vessels of silver and goblets of crystal (15)
قَوَارِيرَا۟ مِن فِضَّةٍ قَدَّرُوهَا تَقْدِيرًا (۱٦)
—crystal of silver— [from] which they will dispense in a precise measure. (16)
وَيُسْقَوْنَ فِيهَا كَأْسًا كَانَ مِزَاجُهَا زَنجَبِيلًا (۱۷)
They will be served therein with a cup of a drink seasoned with Zanjabeel, (17)
عَيْنًا فِيهَا تُسَمَّىٰ سَلْسَبِيلًا (۱۸)
from a spring in it named Salsabeel. (18)

Key Points

«نضرة» means beauty and freshness (or vibrancy). As we read elsewhere: «وجوهٌ یومئذٍ ناضرة»(208) «اَرائك» is the plural of «اَریكة», meaning an adorned couch or throne. «زَمهریر» means intense cold, and «قُطوف» is the plural of «قَطف», meaning the act of picking (fruit). «تذلیل» means to make subservient or to place at one's disposal. «اكواب» is the plural of «كوب», meaning a cup, and «قَواریر» is the plural of «قارورة», meaning glass (or flask), and «سَلسبیل» is the name of a spring in Paradise.

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding the Ayah «جزاهم صبروا»: The reward of the righteous (abrar) is due to the patience they showed in the face of poverty and the calamities of this world.(209)

Just as Hadrat Ali عليه السلام said: «صَبرت و فى العین قذى و فى الحلق شجى »(210) I endured patiently while there was a thorn in my eye and a bone stuck in my throat.

And regarding Imam Husayn عليه السلام, we read: «لقد عجبت من صبرك ملائكة السموات»(211) Indeed, the angels of the heavens were astonished by your patience.

The feeding by the righteous (abrar) was carried out for two goals: «لوجه اللّه» (for the sake of Allah) and «نخاف من ربّنا» (we fear our Lord), and they achieved both goals. «فوقاهم اللّه شرّ ذلك الیوم و لقّاهم نضرةً و سروراً» (So Allah protected them from the evil of that Day, and bestowed upon them radiance and joy.)

The patient ones not only receive reward in the Hereafter, but they also receive divine greetings (salam) and blessings (salawat) in this world. «سلام علیكم بما صبرتم»(212), «بشّر الصّابرین اولئك علیهم صلوات من ربّهم»(213), «انى جزیتهم الیوم بما صبروا»(214)

The blessings of Paradise are of the highest degree of goodness. The tanwin (nunation) in the words «نضرةً», «سروراً», «جنّةً», and «حریراً» signifies grandeur.

208) Al-Qiyamah, 22. 209) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 75, p. 186. 210) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 3. 211) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 98, p. 239. 212) Ar-Ra'd, 24. 213) Al-Baqarah, 155. 214) Al-Mu'minun, 111.

Messages

1- Allah grants the sincere ones their desires. «انّا نخاف... یوماً عبوساً - فوقاهم اللّه شرّ ذلك الیوم»

2- The fruit of fearing Allah is salvation from His wrath. «انّا نخاف... فوقاهم اللّه شرّ ذلك الیوم»

3- Punishment and reward must be commensurate with the deed. (Protecting and saving the deprived leads to man's protection and salvation on the Day of Judgment.) «فوقاهم اللّه شرّ ذلك»

4- Fear today brings security tomorrow, and bringing joy today results in joy tomorrow. «نخاف من ربّنا... فوقاهم اللّه... و لقّاهم نضرة و سرورا»

5- Divine rewards are due to patience. «جزاهم بما صبروا»

6- The people of Paradise live in comfort and tranquility. «متكئین فیها على الارائك»

7- The climate of Paradise is extremely pleasant. «لا یرون فیها شمساً و لا زمهریراً»

8- In Paradise, blessings are subservient to the righteous (abrar). «ذلّلت قطوفها تذلیلاً»

9- The servants of the people of Paradise circle around them like moths. «یطاف علیهم»

10- The vessels of Paradise are made in different shapes and sizes, corresponding to the will and desire of the inhabitants of Paradise. «قَدّروها تقدیراً»

Ayahs

۞ وَيَطُوفُ عَلَيْهِمْ وِلْدَٰنٌ مُّخَلَّدُونَ إِذَا رَأَيْتَهُمْ حَسِبْتَهُمْ لُؤْلُؤًا مَّنثُورًا (۱۹)
They will be waited upon by immortal youths, whom, were you to see them, you will suppose them to be scattered pearls. (19)
وَإِذَا رَأَيْتَ ثَمَّ رَأَيْتَ نَعِيمًا وَمُلْكًا كَبِيرًا (۲٠)
As you look on, you will see there bliss and a great kingdom. (20)
عَـٰلِيَهُمْ ثِيَابُ سُندُسٍ خُضْرٌ وَإِسْتَبْرَقٌ ۖ وَحُلُّوٓا۟ أَسَاوِرَ مِن فِضَّةٍ وَسَقَىٰهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ شَرَابًا طَهُورًا (۲۱)
Upon them will be cloaks of green silk and brocade and they will be adorned with bracelets of silver. Their Lord will give them to drink a pure drink. (21)
إِنَّ هَـٰذَا كَانَ لَكُمْ جَزَآءً وَكَانَ سَعْيُكُم مَّشْكُورًا (۲۲)
[They will be told]: ‘This is your reward, and your efforts have been well-appreciated.’ (22)

Key Points

They asked Imam Kadhim عليه السلام: Why is there no mention of the Hur al-'In (wide-eyed maidens) in this Surah among the rewards for the righteous (Abrar) and the Ahl al-Bayt of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله? The Imam عليه السلام replied: Because of the status of Fatimah Zahra عليها السلام.(215)

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام narrates from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله: When the people of Paradise enter Paradise, they encounter a tree with an expansive shade, and from its base flow two springs. They wash themselves in one spring and drink from the other, and after that, no illness or discomfort will ever afflict their bodies. This is the meaning of «سقاهم ربّهم شراباً طهوراً» (Their Lord will give them to drink a purifying drink).(216)

Heavenly Blessings There are many differences between the blessings of Paradise and the blessings of this world. Here we point to some of them:

They are very abundant: «فاكهة كثیرة» (abundant fruit)(217) They are not seasonal or temporary: «لا مقطوعة» (never ending)(218) There is no prohibition in benefiting from them. «و لا ممنوعة» (nor forbidden)(219) The people of Paradise have the freedom to choose when using them. «مما یتخیرون» (of what they choose)(220) For every kind of appetite, a blessing is available: «ممّا یشتهون» (of what they desire)(221) Using them does not result in ailments or side effects: «لا یُصدّعون» (they will not suffer headache)(222) Benefiting from them is effortless. «و ذلّلت قطوفها تذلیلاً» (and its clusters are brought low, easily reachable)(223) Whatever you wish, servants will provide for you: «و یطوف علیهم» (and there will circulate among them) The servants of Paradise are both beautiful: «لؤلؤاً منثوراً» (scattered pearls), and they do not age: «مخلّدون» (immortal youths) Enjoyment is in complete tranquility: «على الارائك متّكئون» (reclining on couches)(224) Envy and rivalry have no place in Paradise. «نزعنا ما فى صدورهم من غلّ» (We will remove whatever rancor is in their breasts)(225) The blessings are accompanied by the satisfaction and peace of the Lord: «و رضوانٌ من اللّه اكبر» (and Allah's pleasure is greater)(226), «سلام قولاً من ربّ رحیم» (Peace! A word from a Merciful Lord)(227) The believing progeny will join them and enjoy bliss: «الحقنا بهم ذریتهم» (We will join their progeny with them)(228) They become neighbors of the Divine Saints: «و هم جیرانى» (and they are My neighbors)(229) The blessings of Paradise are diverse: «وانهار من لبن... و انهار من عسل...» (and rivers of milk... and rivers of honey...)(230)

215) Manaqib, Vol. 3, p. 328. 216) Bihar, Vol. 8, p. 212. 217) Al-Waqi'ah, 32. 218) Al-Waqi'ah, 33. 219) Al-Waqi'ah, 33. 220) Al-Waqi'ah, 20. 221) Al-Waqi'ah, 21. 222) Al-Waqi'ah, 19. 223) Al-Insan, 14. 224) Ya Sin, 56. 225) Al-Waqi'ah, 16. 226) At-Tawbah, 71. 227) Ya Sin, 58. 228) At-Tur, 21. 229) Du'a Nudbah. 230) Muhammad, 15.

Messages

1- Hospitality in Paradise has special characteristics. Including:

There is no need for asking or requesting, because the servers constantly circulate around the people of Paradise. «یطوف» (circulate)

The hospitality staff have oversight over the people of Paradise from every direction. «یطوف علیهم» (circulate among them)

The servers are youths. «ولدان مخلدون» (immortal youths)(231)

Their appearance is radiant like pearls. «حسبتهم لؤلؤا» (you would think them scattered pearls)

The dominion of the people of Paradise is vast. «ملكاً كبیراً» (a great kingdom)

The color of the servers' clothing is green («خضر») and the material of their clothing is fine silk («سندس») and thick silk («استبرق»).

The cupbearer of the righteous is the Exalted Lord «سقاهم ربّهم» (Their Lord will give them to drink) and their drink is tahur (purifying). «شراباً طهوراً» (a purifying drink). (Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: This drink purifies the hearts of the people of Paradise from everything other than Allah.(232))

2- Although Paradise is a favor and grace from Allah, He considers it the recompense and reward of the righteous so that the people of Paradise may take pride in their deeds. «ان هذا كان لك جزاءً» (Indeed, this is for you a recompense)

3- Divine rewards are based on the intention and quality of the deed, not on the quantity of the work or the amount and value of the charity. (A few pieces of barley bread and all these rewards are solely due to sincerity and self-sacrifice.) «انّ هذا كان لكم جزاءاً و كان سعیكم مشكورا» (Indeed, this is for you a recompense, and your effort has been appreciated)

4- Whoever seeks neither reward nor thanks from people, «لا نرید منكم جزاءً ولا شكوراً» (We desire from you neither reward nor thanks), Allah gives him both reward and thanks. «كان لكم جزاءً و كان سعیكم مشكوراً» (Indeed, this is for you a recompense, and your effort has been appreciated)

5- Thanking and appreciating the efforts of others is a Divine attribute. «سعیكم مشكوراً» (your effort has been appreciated)

6- An insignificant drop of fluid «نطفة امشاج» (a mingled sperm-drop), through faith, action, sincerity, and self-sacrifice, reaches a status where the Creator of existence thanks him. «و كان سعیكم مشكوراً» (and your effort has been appreciated)

231) At-Tur, 24.

232) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Ayahs

إِنَّا نَحْنُ نَزَّلْنَا عَلَيْكَ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ تَنزِيلًا (۲۳)
Indeed We have sent down to you the Quran in a gradual descent. (23)
فَٱصْبِرْ لِحُكْمِ رَبِّكَ وَلَا تُطِعْ مِنْهُمْ ءَاثِمًا أَوْ كَفُورًا (۲٤)
So submit patiently to the judgement of your Lord and do not obey any sinner or ingrate from among them, (24)
وَٱذْكُرِ ٱسْمَ رَبِّكَ بُكْرَةً وَأَصِيلًا (۲٥)
and celebrate the Name of your Lord morning and evening, (25)

Key Points

The term «بُكرةً» refers to the interval between the break of dawn (Fajr) and sunrise. «اَصیل» means the interval from the afternoon (Asr) until sunset, the end of the day. It is possible that the intent of these two words is the remembrance of Allah at the beginning and end of the day, and the establishment of prayer.

Messages

1- Resist the accusation of sorcery and soothsaying leveled against the Quran. «انّا نحن نزلنا علیك القرآن»

2- The Quran was revealed gradually. «نزّلنا علیك القرآن»

3- Implementing the commands of the Quran requires patience (sabr) and steadfastness. «نزّلنا... فاصبر»

4- Steadfastness and patience are valuable when carrying out the command of the Lord, not when insisting upon one's personal preferences. «فاصبر لحكم ربّك»

5- Divine commands are for the growth and development of humanity. «لحكم ربّك»

6- Prophets require Divine admonition. «فاصبر لحكم ربّك»

7- Sinners and transgressors attempt to infiltrate the leadership of the Muslims. «فاصبر لحكم ربّك و لا تطع منهم آثماً او كفوراً»

8- The remembrance of Allah makes a person patient and resilient. «فاصبر... واذكر... بكرة و اصیلاً»

9- Among the suitable times for the remembrance of Allah are the beginning and the end of the day. «بكرة و اصیلاً»

Ayahs

وَمِنَ ٱلَّيْلِ فَٱسْجُدْ لَهُۥ وَسَبِّحْهُ لَيْلًا طَوِيلًا (۲٦)
and worship Him for a watch of the night and glorify Him the night long. (26)
إِنَّ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ يُحِبُّونَ ٱلْعَاجِلَةَ وَيَذَرُونَ وَرَآءَهُمْ يَوْمًا ثَقِيلًا (۲۷)
Indeed they love this transitory life, and disregard a heavy day that is ahead of them. (27)

Key Points

The command for worship and the repetition of the word "Layl" (night) in a short Ayah is a sign of the importance and role of the night in worship. Imam Sadiq and Imam Ridha (عليهما السلام) were asked: What is meant by the command "سبحّه لیلاً طویلاً" (glorify Him a long night)? They replied: The Night Prayer (Salat al-Layl).(233)

233) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Love for and attachment to the world is the source of sin and disbelief (kufr) and is among the signs of habitual sinners. «لا تطع منهم آثماً او كفوراً... انّ هولاء یحبّون العاجلة»

2- The world is fleeting and impermanent. «العاجلة»

3- Worldliness and avoidance of the Hereafter are the basis for habitual sinfulness. «آثما... یحبّون العاجلة و یذرون ورائهم یوماً ثقیلاً»

4- The Day of Judgment (Qiyamah) is an extremely difficult and heavy day. «یوماً ثقیلاً»

5- Attention to the fleeting nature of the world and the difficulty and heaviness of the Day of Judgment are factors that can reduce a person's love for the world. «العاجلة... یوماً ثقیلاً»

Ayahs

نَّحْنُ خَلَقْنَـٰهُمْ وَشَدَدْنَآ أَسْرَهُمْ ۖ وَإِذَا شِئْنَا بَدَّلْنَآ أَمْثَـٰلَهُمْ تَبْدِيلًا (۲۸)
We created them and strengthened their joints, and We will replace them with others like them whenever We like. (28)
إِنَّ هَـٰذِهِۦ تَذْكِرَةٌ ۖ فَمَن شَآءَ ٱتَّخَذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ سَبِيلًا (۲۹)
This is indeed a reminder. So let anyone who wishes take the way toward his Lord. (29)
وَمَا تَشَآءُونَ إِلَّآ أَن يَشَآءَ ٱللَّهُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا (۳٠)
But you will not wish unless it is willed by Allah. Indeed Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (30)
يُدْخِلُ مَن يَشَآءُ فِى رَحْمَتِهِۦ ۚ وَٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ أَعَدَّ لَهُمْ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًۢا (۳۱)
He admits whomever He wishes into His mercy, and He has prepared a painful punishment for the wrongdoers. (31)

Key Points

Asr (أسر) means binding with a rope, and Asir (أسیر) is someone who has been bound. The meaning of "شددنا اسرهم" is that We strengthened the bonds and joints of man.

Messages

1- Disbelief (kufr) and rebellion are the grounds for man's destruction. «آثماً او كفوراً... بدّلنا امثالهم»

2- Allah's ability to create man is a sign of His ability to destroy him. «خلقناهم... بدّلنا امثالهم»

3- Allah has completed the proof (Hujjah) upon the people. «انّ هذه تذكرة»

4- The Quran is a means of awareness (admonition). «انّ هذه تذكرة»

5- The path to Allah is not compulsory; whoever wishes to attain felicity must choose and traverse it himself. «فمن شاء اتخذ الى ربّه سبیلا»

6- Man's will is neither independent of Allah's will nor superior to it. Rather, what man wills is realized only if it is also willed by Allah. «و ما تشاؤون الاّ ان یشاء اللّه»

7- Allah's Will (Mashiyyah) is based on knowledge and wisdom, not frivolity. «یشاء اللّه... علیما حكیما»

8- Based on His wise Will, those whom He wills are encompassed by Divine Mercy. «یدخل من یشاء فى رحمة»

9- Those who are far from Divine Mercy are those who have themselves chosen the path of oppression (zulm). «والظالمین اعدّ لهم عذابا الیما»
